Investigative Information

Assisting Officer

The following was written by ALSPACH, BRENT On Friday, September 24, 2021 at 12:00, ALSPACH, BRENT responded to a crash on Milepost 87.7 I-65 250 ft. At the time of the crash, the weather was clear and the road surface was dry.

Vehicle 1,BLUE Chevrolet Suburban, slowing or stopped in traffic. Vehicle 2, GRAY Ford F-150, slowing or stopped in traffic.

DRIVER 1 STATED THAT BOTH VEHICLES WERE IN THE LEFT LANE OF I-65 NORTHBOUND. HE DID NOT NOTICE TRAFFIC HAD SLOWED DOWN UNTIL THE LAST MINUTE WHEN HE TRIED TO CHANGE LANES TO THE RIGHT.

DRIVER 2 STATED BOTH VEHICLES WERE IN THE LEFT LANE OF I-65 NORTH WHEN TRAFFIC IN FRONT OF HIM WAS SLOWING DOWN DUE TO ACONSTRUCTION WORKER ON THE RIGHT SHOULDER WAVING TO SLOW TRAFFIC DOWN. AS HE SLOWED DOWN THAT IS WHEN VEHICLE 1 STRUCK HIM FROM BEHIND.

SINCE TRAFFIC WAS SLOWING DOWN AT THE REQUEST OF THE CONSTRUCTION IN THE AREAAND VEHICLE 2 DID SLOW DOWN. VEHICLE 1 WAS TOO CLOSE TO VEHICLE 2 TO MAKE ALANE CHANGE TO THE RIGHT TO AVOID STRIKING VEHICLE 2 IN

THE RIGHT REAR WITH THE LEFT FRONT.

the scope of this Prompt Care.

Motor Vehicle Accident: General Precautions

Strong forces may be involved in a car accident. It is important to watch for any new symptoms that may signal hidden injury.

It is normal to feel sore and tight in your muscles and back the next day, and not just the muscles you initially injured. Remember, all the parts of your body are connected, so while initially one area hurts, the next day another may hurt. Also, when you injure yourself, it causes inflammation, which then causes the muscles to tighten up and hurt more. After the initial worsening, it should gradually i improve over the next few days. However, more severe pain should be reported.

Even without a definite head injury, you can still get a concussion from your head suddenly jerking forward, backward or sideways when falling. Concussions and even bleeding can still occur, especially if you have had a recent injury or take blood thinner. It is common to have a mild headache and fee! tired and even nauseous or dizzy.

A motor vehicle accident, even a minor one, can be very stressful and cause emotional or mental symptoms after the event. These may include:

General sense of anxiety and fear

Recurring thoughts or nightmares about the accident

Trouble sleeping or changes in appetite

Feeling depressed, sad or low in energy

Irritable or easily upset

Feeling the need to avoid activities, places or people that remind you of the accident Printed on 1/$/22 9:20 AM Page 1

Patient Instructions by Waugh, Darcy Ann, DO at 9/24/2021 7:15 PM (continued)

Tn most cases, these are normal reactions and are not severe enough to get in the way of your usual activities. These feelings usually go away within a few days, or sometimes after a few weeks.

Home care Muscle pain, sprains and strains

Even if you have no visible injury, it is not unusual to be sore all over, and have new aches and pains the first couple of days after an accident. Take it easy at first, and don't over do it.

* Initially, don't try to stretch out the sore spots. If there is a strain, stretching may make it worse. Massage may help relax the muscles without stretching them,

e You can use an ice pack or cold compress on and off to the sore spots 10 to 20 minutes at a time, as often as you feel comfortable, This may help reduce the inflammation, swelling and pain. You can make an ice pack by wrapping a plastic bag of ice cubes or crushed ice in a thin towel or using a bag of frozen peas or corn.

Wound care

e If you have any scrapes or abrasions, they usually heal within 10 days. It is important to keep the abrasions clean while they first start to heal. However, an infection may occur even with proper care, so watch for early signs of infection such as:

© Increasing redness or swelling around the wound © Increased warmth of the wound O Red streaking lines away from the wound © Draining pus Medicines

e Talk to your healthcare provider before taking new medicines, especially if you have other medical problems or are taking other medicines.

e If you need anything for pain, you can take acetaminophen or ibuprofen, unless you were given a different pain medicine to use. Talk with your healthcare provider before using these medicines if you have chronic liver or kidney disease, or ever had a stomach ulcer or gastrointestinal bleeding, or are taking blood thinner medicines.

e Be careful if you are given prescription pain medicines, narcotics, or medicine for muscle spasm. They can make you sleepy, dizzy and can affect your coordination, reflexes and judgment. Don't drive or do work where you can injure yourself when taking them.

Follow-up care

Follow up with your healthcare provider, or as advised, If emotional or mental symptoms last more than 3 weeks, follow up with your healthcare provider. You may have a more serious traumatic stress reaction. There are treatments that can help. If you had a concussion, be sure you or a friend writes down any instructions if you are still dazed or confused.

If X-rays or CT scans were done, you will be notified if there are any concerns that affect your treatment. Call 911

Call 911 if any of these occur:

* Confused or difficulty arousing e Fainting or loss of consciousness * Rapid heart rate * Trouble with speech or vision, weakness of an arm or leg or, if one pupil of your eye becomes larger than the other * Trouble walking or talking, loss of balance, numbness or weakness in one side of your body, facial droop When to seek medical advice

Call your healthcare provider right away if any of the following occur:

e New or worsening headache or vision problems e New or worsening neck, back, abdomen, arm or leg pain e Nausea or vomiting ¢ Dizziness or vertigo e Redness, swelling, or pus coming from any wound ay

When one car hits another, each person’s body is thrown toward the impact, then away from it. This ts whiplash. Even at slow speeds, the force puts stress and strain on the spine. This is especially true for the neck. The weight of the head stretches and damages muscles and ligaments. It may pull spinal bones out of line.

The bones that protect your spinal cord (vertebrae) can be forced out of position. Disks are the spine’s shock absorbers. They can bulge, rupture, or wear down, Nerves can get pinched or inflamed, And muscles and ligaments can be stretched or torn.

Symptoms of whiplash

A wide. array of symptoms. can follow an auto accident. Symptoms may appear right away, Or they may be delayed for several days. Symptoms may include:

Patient Instructions by Waugh, Darcy Ann, DO at 9/24/2021 7:15 PM (continued) e Pain, especially in your neck, shoulder, arm, or lower back

e¢ Arm or leg numbness e Stiffness * Headache e Dizziness Treating whiplash

You may be asked to do one or more of the following:

Ice the injured area for 24 to 48 hours. Do this for 20 minutes. Repeat 5 times a day.

After 48 hours, put moist heat on the injured area for 20 minutes. Repeat 5 times a day.

Wear a cervical collar for as long as recommended,

Take nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory (NSAIDs) medicines or muscle relaxants as directed by your healthcare provider

Subjective:

HPI

Daniel Voik, 57 y.o. male, is here for evaluation of headache, left shoulder discomfort and right hip pain following and motor vehicle accident at 11:00 a.m. today in Indianapolis where he was a restrained driver. He reports that he was driving on his way from Kentucky to visit his mom in the nursing home where he had to make an appointment to see her. As traffic with slowing down to approximately 45 mph of the interstate another vehicle coming behind him did not slow down and hit the rear end of his pickup truck at about 80 mph. His airbags did not deploy, he did not strike his head on any of the objects would in the pickup CAB and his seatbelt maintained intact. Since that time he has had a headache blood located on the left temporal and parietal side of his head denies any posterior neck pain or restricted range of motion. He has not had any increase in the headache an infected has subsided slightly. He denies any visual changes, numbness or tingling, weakness of extremities, confusion, dizziness, altered speech, nausea or vomiting. He feels as if the shoulder is bruised from the seatbelt and his hip is bruised from the locking point of the seatbelt. He does not have any restricted range of motion of any of his extremities.

Review of Systems Constitutional: Negative.

41/09/2021 - Telephone in BAPTIST HEALTH MEDICAL GROUP SPORTS MEDICINE (continued) Instructions for After Discharge

Patient Instructions

None

Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding

Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye

Sudden severe headache with no known cause

Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a stroke.

For more information: American Stroke Association 1-888-4-STROKE www.strokeassociation.org

IF YOU SMOKE, VAPE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:

Why is smoking bad for me?

Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you smoke, STOP!

For more information: Quit Now Kentucky 1-800-QUIT-NOW

Instructions for After Discharge

Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding

Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye

Sudden severe headache with no known cause

Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a stroke.

For more information: American Stroke Association 1-888-4-STROKE www.strokeassociation.org

IF YOU SMOKE, VAPE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:

Why is smoking bad for me?

Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you smoke, STOP!

For more information:

Quit Now Kentucky 1-800-QUIT-NOW https://kentucky.quitiogix.org/en-US/

If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! e Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

~~ Author: Pitcock, Christopher V., MD ~ Service: — _ Author Type: Physician Filed: 10/26/21 1229 Encounter Date: [DOB-1957] Creation Time: 10/26/21 1118 Status: Signed Editor: Pitcock, Christopher V., MD (Physician)

Chief Complaint

Neck Pain (Neck pain since MVA on 24th of September), Back Pain (lumbothoracic back pain since MVA), Shoulder Pain (Left shoulder pain with paresethesia since MVA), Hip Pain (right hip pain, more in si joint. Since MVA), and Migraine (Headaches since MVA)

Subjective

Daniel W Volk presents to BAPTIST HEALTH MEDICAL GROUP SPORTS MEDICINE History of Present Illness 1. Neck pain. Rear ended MVA, getting PT and helping some 2. LBP-From MVA without radiation in PT helping some 3. Left shoulder pain- occ sharp pain around the trap area with radiation down to L forearm, along with "tingling" and cold sensation. But only usually with shoulder abduction and external rotation. 4. Rhip pain- better but hasn't been addressed in PT as of yet 5. Bilat occipital pain with radiation in hat band dist. Objective Vital Signs: BP 142/88 (BP Location: Left arm, Patient Position: Sitting, Cuff Size: Adult) | Pulse 74 | Temp 97 °F (36.1 °C) | Resp 16 | Ht 180.3 cm (71") | Wt 97.5 kg (215 Ib) | SpO2 98% | BMI 29.99 kg/m? Physical Exam Vitals reviewed. Constitutional: Appearance: He is well-developed. HENT: Head: Normocephalic and atraumatic. Eyes: Conjunctiva/sclera: Conjunctivae normal. Pupils: Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light. Cardiovascular:

Comments: No peripheral edema Pulmonary:

Effort: Pulmonary effort is normal.

Musculoskeletal:

Comments: Tightness of the trapezius and levators bilateral left greater than right. Cervical spine with good range of motion, negative Spurling. Left shoulder with full painless range of motion. DTRs 1+ symmetric. Motor 5 out of 5.

Skin: General: Skin is warm and dry.

Neurological: Mental Status: He is alert and oriented to person, place, and time.

Psychiatric: Behavior: Behavior normal.

Assessment and Plan Diagnoses and all orders for this visit:

1. Bilateral occipital neuralgia (Primary) - predniSONE (DELTASONE) 10 MG tablet; 6 tabs qd x 3 d, 4 tabs qd x 3d, 3 tabs qd x 3d, 2 tabs qd x 3. d,1 tab qd x 3d Dispense: 48 tablet; Refill: 0

2. Strain of neck muscle, initial encounter - predniSONE (DELTASONE) 10 MG tablet; 6 tabs qd x 3 d, 4 tabs qd x 3d, 3 tabs qd x 3d, 2 tabs qdx 3.d,1 tab qd x 3d Dispense: 48 tablet; Refill: 0

3. Cervical radiculitis - predniSONE (DELTASONE) 10 MG tablet; 6 tabs qd x 3 d, 4 tabs qd x 3d, 3 tabs qd x 3d, 2 tabs qd x 3.d,1 tab qd x 3d Dispense: 48 tablet; Refill: 0

Says if he may be having some left-sided cervical radicular symptoms however negative Spurling and placing his left hand behind his head did not seem to make a difference. Could also be nerve involvement because of the muscle tightness.

Will continue with PT add prednsione and FU 2 weeks.

Follow Up

Return in about 2 weeks (around 11/9/2021).

Patient was given instructions and counseling regarding his condition or for health maintenance advice. Please see specific information pulled into the AVS if appropriate.

Instructions for After Discharge

Patient Instructions

None

Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding

Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye

Sudden severe headache with no known cause

Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a stroke.

For more information: American Stroke Association 1-888-4-STROKE www.strokeassociation.org

IF YOU SMOKE, VAPE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:

Why is smoking bad for me?

Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you smoke, STOP!

For more information: Quit Now Kentucky 1-800-QUIT-NOW

None Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye

Sudden severe headache with no known cause Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

Phone : [PHONE-1808] Electronically signed by: Michael Gilbert, PA-C on 2022-12-22 08:22 AM

CC The patient is in today for followup. He had a motor vehicle injury in September of 2021.

Subjective He had an incidental note of a brain tumor meningioma that he had resected in June of this year and he is doing well with that. He is very pleased. He states that his headaches have resolved since the brain tumor was removed. We have given him an injection in the left shoulder subacromial space for some impingement that he had. That is also doing much better. He does have some bad days as far as some left-sided neck pain. He does occasionally have some bad days as far as his shoulder is concerned, but otherwise he is very pleased. He is now greater than 12 months from the injury.

ROS A 10-point review of systems was obtained. All systems reviewed were negative except as noted above.

PSHx Ankle surgery - , right ankle 2013

Soc Hx No illicit drug use Does not drink Does not smoke Is not pregnant

AH Procedures

MUSCULOSKELETAL: He has 5/5 strength in biceps, triceps, and forearm strength testing. Negative Hoffmann bilaterally. He does have full range of motion of the shoulder with excellent strength. He does have positive Neer that is mildly positive.

1. Left shoulder impingement and strain, basically back to baseline and at maximal medical improvement.

2. Cervical sprain and strain with some degenerative changes noted, basically back to baseline.

3. Incidental note of a brain mass.

4. AC joint arthritis.

5. Meningioma that was an incidental finding on brain MRI being treated by Norton Neuroscience Institute.

6. Mild facet arthritis and mild disc disease.

7. Headaches.

8. Lumbar sprain and strain.

Plan At this point, | do believe that Mr. Volk is at maximal medical improvement. He understands that he may need futures to include some physical therapy and/or injections for a flare-up of his shoulder and/or his neck. We will have him continue to work with Dr. Sun as far as his brain tumor is concerned, and we will see him back in the office PRN.

Phone : [PHONE-430] Electronically signed by: Michael Gilbert, PA-C on 2022-01-04 02:33 PM

Neck pain and shoulder pain.

The patient is in today for followup from a motor vehicle injury. He has had physical therapy. His neck is feeling a lot better. His range of motion is improved. He is having no radicular symptoms at this time. He does still have some discomfort with abduction of the shoulder. He had a steroid injection placed in the subacromial space by me on his last visit. He got some relief of his symptoms but he is still not where he is happy with his left shoulder pain.

A 10-point review of systems was obtained. All systems reviewed were negative, except as noted above.

At this point, we will have him continue with his current physical therapy. Follow up with neurosurgery as the appointment is indicated. We have also discussed the potential for an injection today. He wanted to go ahead and proceed with that, and then will see him back in the office in 4 weeks.

After informed consent was obtained under bedside sterile technique and after an appropriate time-out was taken, the left subacromial space was injected with 2 cc of 1% plain lidocaine and 40 mg Kenalog. He tolerated the procedure well. Post-injection instructions were understood. We will see him back in the office in 4 weeks.

HISTORY: Pt has had pain in Left Shoulder with decreased ROM since MVA 9/24/2021. No history of surgery

COMPARISON: None TECHNIQUE: Routine MR sequences were obtained in 3 planes.

FINDINGS:

GH joint and Labrum: A small effusion contains no loose bodies. There are no gienohumeral joint degenerative changes. There is a broad partial-thickness tear at the base of the superior labrum. There is also a small tear at the base of the inferior labrum. The rest of the labrum is normal in appearance.

AC joint and Impingement: Increased fluid and synovium moderately expand the joint. There is no Widening of the joint or disruption of the coracoclavicular ligaments. Moderate marrow edema is present in the distal clavicle with mild edema in the distal acromion. The findings are consistent with a moderate active AC arthropathy which could represent a primary source for pain. There is deformity of the underlying supraspinatus.

The acromiohumeral space is not narrowed by any abnormality or anomaly of the distal acromion. There is no thickening of the coracoacromial ligament. The distance between the coracoid and humeral head is not narrowed,

Rotator Cuff: Severe supraspinatus degeneration/injury is not associated with partial or full- thickness tearing. The infraspinatus, teres minor and subscapularis muscles and tendons are normal.

Bursa: There is fluid in the subdeltoid portion.

Biceps, Deltoid, other muscles: Signal change in the intra-articular portion of the biceps tendon

18 consistent with mild degeneration/injury. The descending portion is normal, and there is no tearing. The deltoid and other musculotendinous structures are normal.

Daily Note

Diagnoses Spine MS54.2 Cervicalgia S33.5XXD_ Sprain of ligaments of lumbar spine, subsequent encounter S$16.1XXD_ Strain of muscle, fascia and tendon at neck level, subsequent encounter

Subjective Examination

Mechanism of Injury: Primary Episode: Date of Injury:

* Date [DOB-1760]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

ADL / Functional Status: Current Status: ADL/ IADL: Activities:

¢ Activity 1(75% improved) difficulty sitting ¢ Activity 2 difficulty checking blind spot ¢ Activity 3 difficulty bending over

Questionnaires: Focus on Therapeutic Outcomes (FOTO): Physical Functional Status Measure: Intake: Patient: ¢ Score(58 at IE) 59 Chief Complaint: Pain: Severity: ¢ Current(back 3/10) 4/10 ¢ At Worst 4/10 Daily Comments: ¢ Got a shoulder cortisone shot yesterday and has partial tears. Neck is doing okay. Had a gripping feeling in his neck yesterday.

Objective Examination

Observations: * severe forward head/shoulder posture Functional Tests: ¢ moderate motor control during cervical rotation; non-fluid motion of neck

SH rhythm: Increased UT activation on L>R; moderate eccentric control Range of Motion: Spine: Pre-Treatment: Active Cervical (%):

Subjective Examination

Mechanism of Injury: Primary Episode: Date of Injury:

* Date [DOB-1740]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

ADL / Functional Status: Current Status: ADL/ IADL: Activities:

¢ Activity | difficulty sitting ¢ Activity 2 difficulty checking blind spot ¢ Activity 3 difficulty bending over

Questionnaires: Focus on Therapeutic Outcomes (FOTO): Physical Functional Status Measure: Intake: Patient: * Score 58

Chief Complaint: Pain: Severity: ¢ Current 0/10 ¢ At Worst 7/10

Objective Examination

Observations: * severe forward head/shoulder posture Functional Tests: * poor motor control during cervical rotation; non-fluid motion of neck

Diagnoses Spine MS54.2 Cervicalgia S33.5XXD_ Sprain of ligaments of lumbar spine, subsequent encounter S$16.1XXD_ Strain of muscle, fascia and tendon at neck level, subsequent encounter

Subjective Examination

Mechanism of Injury: Primary Episode: Date of Injury:

* Date [DOB-1720]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

ADL / Functional Status: Current Status: ADL/ IADL: Activities:

¢ Activity 1(75% improved) difficulty sitting ¢ Activity 2 difficulty checking blind spot ¢ Activity 3 difficulty bending over

Questionnaires: Focus on Therapeutic Outcomes (FOTO): Physical Functional Status Measure: Intake: Patient: ¢ Score(58 at IE) 59 Chief Complaint: Pain: Severity: ¢ Current(back 3/10) 4/10 ¢ At Worst 4/10 Daily Comments: ¢ Neck is doing okay. Neck feels limited in ROM and feels stiff, but is not having any sharp pain. Had some pain in his back when he stood for an hour yesterday.

Objective Examination

Observations: * severe forward head/shoulder posture Functional Tests: ¢ moderate motor control during cervical rotation; non-fluid motion of neck

SH rhythm: Increased UT activation on L>R; moderate eccentric control Range of Motion: Spine: Pre-Treatment: Active Cervical (%):

Mechanism of Injury: Primary Episode: Date of Injury:

* Date [DOB-1705]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

ADL / Functional Status: Current Status: ADL/ IADL: Activities:

¢ Lifting(80% improved) difficulty with L UE

* Reaching(80% improved) difficulty with L shoulder

¢ Activity 1(75% improved) difficulty sitting

* Activity 2(80% improved) difficulty checking blind spot ¢ Activity 3(80% improved) difficulty bending over

Questionnaires: Focus on Therapeutic Outcomes (FOTO): Physical Functional Status Measure: Intake: Patient: ¢ Score(58 at IE) 61 Chief Complaint: Pain: Severity: ¢ Current(sholder) 3/10 ¢ At Worst(shoulder) 5/10 Daily Comments: ¢ Neck is doing okay. Feels more loose but has a pop when he looks to the L. Gets a pinch in his mid-back intermittently. Shoulder is doing okay, but had difficulty lifting any weight. Feels 90% improved overall. A lot of the pain is gone, just has a pop. Shoulder starts to ache when not moving long enough.

Pt presented to therapy reporting his neck feeling more stiff this morning and thinks he may be over doing his HEP exercises. Reviewed pt's form and modified to be pain free range and decreased intensity. MT focused on upper cervical mobility with noted mild to moderate decrease in suboccipital tone bilaterally. Continue with POC and progress as tolerated.

Diagnoses Spine MS54.2 Cervicalgia S33.5XXD_ Sprain of ligaments of lumbar spine, subsequent encounter S$16.1XXD_ Strain of muscle, fascia and tendon at neck level, subsequent encounter

Subjective Examination

Mechanism of Injury: Primary Episode: Date of Injury:

* Date [DOB-1700]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

ADL / Functional Status: Current Status: ADL/ IADL: Activities:

¢ Activity | difficulty sitting ¢ Activity 2 difficulty checking blind spot ¢ Activity 3 difficulty bending over

Questionnaires: Focus on Therapeutic Outcomes (FOTO): Physical Functional Status Measure: Intake: Patient: * Score 58 Chief Complaint: Pain: Severity: ¢ Current 0/10 ¢ At Worst 7/10 Daily Comments: ¢ Overdid it on Wednesday and had more pain in his back and neck.

Objective Examination

Observations: * severe forward head/shoulder posture Functional Tests: * poor motor control during cervical rotation; non-fluid motion of neck

SH rhythm: Increased UT activation on L>R; poor eccentric control Range of Motion: Spine: Pre-Treatment: Active Cervical (%):

* Date [DOB-1680]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

¢ The pt demonstrates 75% progress towards goals. He demonstrates improved cervical ROM and improving posture. He has improved tolerance to sleep, driving, and ADLs. He will benefit from continued skilled PT to progress shoulder strengthening and to improved SH rhythm and deep cervical motor control.

In my professional opinion, this client requires skilled physical therapy in conjunction with a home exercise program to address the problems and achieve the goals outlined below. Overall rehabilitation potential is good. The patient has been educated regarding their diagnosis, prognosis and related pathology. The patient exhibits good understanding and performance of the therapeutic activity/instructions outlined during this skilled rehabilitation session.

Problems & Goals

Problem #1 Functional Tests: moderate motor control during cervical rotation; non-fluid motion of neck-50% improved

SH rhythm: Increased UT activation on L>R; moderate eccentric control-50% improved LTG Achieve by Jan 18, 2022. Progress: Good progress. Diagnoses Spine MS54.2 Cervicalgia S33.5XXD_ Sprain of ligaments of lumbar spine, subsequent encounter S16.1XXD_ Strain of muscle, fascia and tendon at neck level, subsequent encounter

Subjective Examination

Mechanism of Injury: Primary Episode: Date of Injury:

* Date [DOB-1660]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

ADL/ Functional Status: Current Status: ADL/ Initial Current

TADL: Activities: Oct 12, 2021 Nov 24, 2021

* Activity 1(75% improved) difficulty sitting difficulty sitting

* Activity 2 difficulty checking blind spot @ difficulty checking blind spot

* Activity 3 difficulty bending over 4 difficulty bending over

Oct 12, 2021 Nov 24, 2021 * Current(back 3/10) 3/10 4/10 1/10 ¢ At Worst 7/10 4/10 -3/10

Daily Comments: ¢ Pt reports finding of brain tumor size of a quarter. Has pain in shoulder still, but neck is still painful. Feels 75% better overall. Biting shoulder pain is less frequent, but is still intense. Still has sensation down his L arm but is less frequent. Gets cold sensation 2-3 times a day.

Subjective Examination

Mechanism of Injury: Primary Episode: Date of Injury:

* Date [DOB-1645]

Mechanism of Injury:

* The pt is a 57 yo male with c/o pain in his neck secondary to MVA on 9/24/2021. He was rear ended on the highway. Went to immediate care and cleared of head trauma. He was moving when he was hit from behind while moving, when the car was going about 55 MPH. Has had neck and back pain since. Has had pain in his L shoulder and forearm. Feels cold and numb. Arm pain has been increasing over the last few days. Has had HA since accident. Pain is mostly on the L, and intermittently gets HA on the R. Feels popping and clicking in his neck and back. Pain into his low back. Has discomfort into his R hip. Pain in his back worsens with prolonged sitting or standing. Pain constant through the day. Has been on anti-inflammatory for 1 week.

ADL / Functional Status: Current Status: ADL/ IADL: Activities:

¢ Activity | difficulty sitting ¢ Activity 2 difficulty checking blind spot ¢ Activity 3 difficulty bending over

Questionnaires: Focus on Therapeutic Outcomes (FOTO): Physical Functional Status Measure: Intake: Patient: * Score 58 Chief Complaint: Pain: Severity: ¢ Current 0/10 ¢ At Worst 7/10 Daily Comments: * Doing okay. Still sore, and saw MD. Going to start taking an stronger anti-inflammatory.

Objective Examination

Observations: * severe forward head/shoulder posture Functional Tests: * poor motor control during cervical rotation; non-fluid motion of neck

SH rhythm: Increased UT activation on L>R; poor eccentric control Range of Motion: Spine: Pre-Treatment: Active Cervical (%):

The pt demonstrates signs and sx consistent with WAD secondary to MVA. He demonstrates decreased cervical and shoulder ROM with muscle guarding, cervical segmental hypomobility, and altered posture. He exhibits lumbar ROM deficits. These deficits limit his tolerance to sitting, bending, and driving. The pt will benefit form skilled PT to address these deficits.

In my professional opinion, this client requires skilled physical therapy in conjunction with a home exercise program to address the problems and achieve the goals outlined below. Overall rehabilitation potential is good. The patient has been educated regarding their diagnosis, prognosis and related pathology. The patient exhibits good understanding and performance of the therapeutic activity/instructions outlined during this skilled rehabilitation session.

Problems & Goals

Problem #1 Functional Tests: poor motor control during cervical rotation; non-fluid motion of neck

Dear Pitcock, Christopher V, Md 2400 Eastpoint Pkwy

Ste 110

Louisville, KY 40223

| had the great pleasure of seeing your patient, Daniel Volk, at Norton Healthcare's multidisciplinary Brain Tumor Center.

In summary, Mr. Volk has a right parietal lesion most consistent with a meningioma discovered upon a noncontrasted MRI. This lesion causes a mild amount of T2/flair signal change. This lesion was discovered upon headaches after motor vehicle accident. Patient does report intermittent left hemibody sensation changes most predominant in the leg. | recommended Keppra for possible focal seizures, but the patient declines at this time. We will obtain a contrasted MRI/MRV and plan on seeing the patient back in the office to discuss further treatment options.

Ala. SISK P4-¢

Real people. Remarkable care.

Patient ID: Daniel Volk DOB: 2/22/1964

Referring Provider: Pitcock, Christopher V,* PCP: Pitcock, Christopher V, MD

Chief Complaint: The patient presents in consultation at the request of Pitcock, Christopher V,* for a chief complaint of a brain lesion.

History of Present Illness:

Mr. Volk is a 57 yr/o male who presents to me for evaluation of a brain lesion. The following history was obtained from patient and spouse, as well as medical records available for review. The patient initially presented with headaches after a motor vehicle accident. The patient was hit from behind in September of this year on the interstate. He was restrained and denies any head trauma. He reports that since then, he has had intermittent headaches that have been somewhat interfering with his normal life. He denies any other associated symptoms with these headaches. For further work-up because of these headaches, he underwent a noncontrasted MRI scan in November which revealed a right parietal lesion along the vertex measuring approximately 2 cm with associated T2/flair signal intensity. The patient denies any focal weakness, but does report that he had a rotator cuff injury on the left during the accident. He does report some left hemibody sensation changes most predominant in the left lower extremity. He reports that it feels like a warm sensation going down the back of his left leg. He denies any involuntary/uncontrollable shaking or other definitive seizure activity. For the findings of the MRI, patient was referred to our office for further evaluation. The patient does not have any history of cancer in the past.

Allergies, Medications and Past History: The following portions of the patient's history were reviewed and updated as appropriate: allergies, current medications, past family history, past medical history, past social history, past surgical history and problem list.

Diagnostic Studies:

Review of imaging: | have personally reviewed and interpreted the MRI. My findings are described in the above HPI. Review of radiology test reports: | have personally reviewed the radiology test reports associated with the above images, and they support my findings.

Assessment and Plan:

In summary, Mr. Volk has a right parietal lesion most consistent with a meningioma discovered upon a noncontrasted MRI. This lesion causes a mild amount of T2/flair signal change. This lesion was discovered upon headaches after motor vehicle accident. Patient does report intermittent left hemibody sensation changes most predominant in the leg. | recommended Keppra for possible focal seizures, but the patient declines at this time. We will obtain a contrasted MRI/MRV and plan on seeing the patient back in the office to discuss further treatment options.

It was a pleasure meeting Mr. Volk today. Please call my office with any questions or concerns.

Patient ID: Daniel W Volk DOB: 2/22/1964

Chief Complaint: Brain Tumor (fu after MRI) History of Present Illness:

Daniel W Volk is a 57 yr/o male who presents today for routine follow-up. Daniel was found to have a lesion located in the right parietal lobe. The lesion has the appearance of a possible meningioma.

He is doing fairly well. He complains of headaches. He also complains of vision problems. He has intermittent paresthesias described as a feeling of warmth in his left leg that are intermittent. He has previously declined a trial of Keppra.

Allergies, Medications and Past History:

The following portions of the patient's history were reviewed and updated as appropriate: allergies, current medications, past family history, past medical history, past social history, past surgical history and problem list.

parafalcine parietal lesion consistent with meningioma with invasion of superior sagittal sinus, brain compression, and cerebral edema.

Review of radiology test reports: | have personally reviewed the radiology test reports associated with the above images, and they support my findings.

| reviewed these results with the patient.

Assessment and Plan:

Overall, Daniel W Volk has a lesion consistent with meningioma.

Treatment options at this time include: Continued surveillance.

Craniotomy for the purpose of diagnosis and maximal safe resection of the lesion. The potential benefits and risks of craniotomy were discussed at length. Risks include, but are not limited to, death, coma, paralysis, stroke, hemorrhage, damage to the senses, memory, cognition, language ability, need for tracheostomy or gastrostomy, meningitis, wound infection requiring bone flap removal, persistent pain, CSF leak, poor cosmetic result, and other risks that cannot be disclosed at this time due to their unforeseen nature. In addition, we discussed the fact that because of the nature of brain tumors, a degree of tumor tissue is most often left behind. Further treatment options, if needed, will be dictated by the tissue diagnosis.

Radiation therapy. A detailed discussion of the potential benefits and risks was deferred to my colleague, Dr. Spalding, who will discuss the different radiation therapy options.

He will contact me with a decision on treatment plan.

Thank you for allowing me to participate in the care of your patient. Please contact me with any questions or concerns.

Dear fellow providers,

| saw your patient, Daniel W Volk, for his diagnosis with associated orders of Problem List Items Addressed This Visit Meningioma (CMS/HCC) - Primary

| saw the patient today along with his wife at the above location. Relevant history includes, as per Dr. Sun, that the patient has a "right parafalcine parietal lesion consistent with meningioma with a small amount of cerebral edema as well as invasion of the superior sagittal sinus. He has headaches as well as intermittent paresthesias in his left lower extremity concerning for possible seizure."

The lesion was found upon a noncontrasted MRI, which was taken when the patient was experiencing headaches after a motor vehicle accident. The patient previously declined Keppra. He mentions he is on medications for his blood pressure and gout, and he denies a history of seizures.

[DOB-1489]: MRI Venogram Brain Wo Contrast (ACCESSION NUMBER: EPD22MRI7216)

IMPRESSION:

Focal absence of flow related signal involving the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor demonstrated on the MRI brain from the same day. Small surrounding collateral vessel formation. Constellation of findings is

suspicious for local tumor invasion/thrombosis. The remainder of the superior sagittal sinus maintains flow related signal.

Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portions of the right transverse sinus which may reflect slow flow Generated on 12/5/23 1:44 PM Page 40




NORTON CANCER INSTITUTE Volk, Daniel W IN NORTON 315 East Broadway MRN: EP01968571, DOB: 2/22/1964, Sex: M

Real people. Remarkable care.

associated with a nondominant sinus although a component of chronic dural venous sinus thrombosis/stenosis not entirely excluded. There is no

clear evidence for acute dural venous sinus thrombosis based on the MRV and corresponding brain MRI. No parenchymal infarcts.

On my interpretation, | agree with the above.

[DOB-1487]: MRI Brain Wo & W Con (ACCESSION NUMBER: EPD22MRI7222)

IMPRESSION:

Enhancing posterior right parafalcine extra-axial lesion causes mild local mass effect and parenchymal edema on the adjacent posterior right parietal lobe. There is possible local invasion of the superior sagittal sinus at this level by enhancing tumor.

The imaging findings statistically most likely represent a meningioma although other neoplasms could have a similar appearance. If there is any older neuroimaging available, this would be helpful to confirm stability of these findings. On my interpretation, | agree with the above.

Relevant physical examination includes ECOG performance status of 0, and he is in no acute distress. Mentation, cranial nerves 2-12, strength, sensation and gait are intact.

| discussed with the patient and his wife the relevant imaging findings, the tumor pathology, the risks/benefits/alternatives to management including radiation therapy, and | answered all parties' questions.

Your patient wishes to proceed as follows:

1) The patient will discuss options of resection versus observation versus empiric radiation with his family. My recommendation is that he proceed with surgical resection. He may seek a second opinion, prior to making any decision. He will contact us should he wish to proceed.

| will Keep you apprised of his progress, and please do not hesitate to contact me with any questions.

Yours,

surgery scheduling and follow up questions

From To Sent Volk, Daniel W P Nmg Nni Neurosurgery Brownsboro 4/26/2022 9:21 PM Nurse (supporting David A. Sun, MD)

| would like to proceed with removal of the meningioma and have reached out to your office for scheduling the week of June 13 if possible. Will | need another MRI/MRV? Is 100% removal of my tumor not possible because it is attached to the main vein? What meds will | take post surgery? Will | have to be on anti-seizure meds and for how long? How long will | be in hospital/renab? What is my chance of stroke during surgery? What are driving and flying restrictions post surgery? Kopp, Kellie, RN at 6/28/2022 0946

Author: Kopp, Kellie, RN Service: — Author Type: Registered Nurse Filed: 6/28/2022 9:46 AM Encounter Date: 6/28/2022 Status: Signed Editor: Kopp, Kellie, RN (Registered Nurse)

Spoke with patient for post-op follow-up. Pt states that he is feeling pretty good. He reports that he has had some fatigue but he is out and walking daily with wife. He reports that his appetite is down and that he is not sleeping too well at night. Navigator let him know that this should improve when off of steroids. He reports that he is washing hair daily with baby shampoo and is not having an issues with his incision. Post-op appointment reviewed and patient encouraged to call with any questions or needs.

Patient ID: Daniel W Volk DOB: 2/22/1964

Chief Complaint: Brain Tumor (BT po 6/21)

History of Present Illness: Daniel W Volk is a 58 yr/o male who presents today for routine follow-up.

Daniel has undergone the following therapies: craniotomy for meningioma WHO grade 1 on 6/21/2022.

He is doing fairly well. He does have new complaints. He has some dizziness when he stands up. The warm sensation on the left side has improved.

Allergies, Medications and Past History: The following portions of the patient's history were reviewed and updated as appropriate: allergies, current medications, past family history, past medical history, past social history, past surgical history and problem list.

HIPAA COMPLIANT MEDICAL AUTHORIZATION

(Authorization to Release Protected Health Care Information)

To: Norton Neuroscience Institute 4915 Norton Healthcare Blvd., Suite 301 Louisville, KY 40241

Dear Medical Records Clerk:

I have retained The Whaley Law Firm, PSC to represent me in matters related to an injury sustained due to a third parties’ negligence. Please forward a copy of any & all records not previously provided to The Whaley Law Firm upon request.

Pursuant to the Health Insurance Portability & Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy Regulations, 45 CFR 164.508, the provider listed above is hereby authorized to release to The Whaley Law Firm, PSC or any of their representatives, all medical records, including but not limited to: Office Notes, History, Physical, Consultation Notes, Discharge Summaries, Orders, Progress Notes, Laboratory Results, Nurses’ Notes, Emergency Room Records, Operative Reports, In-Patient Records and films of X-Rays, MRIs, CT Scans, or PET Scans, Pharmacy and Drug Records, Medical Bills and Health Insurance Information, Medicaid/Medicare Records, concerning ANY medical treatment that I have received from you and/or at your facility, as well as ALL such records which you keep in the regular course of business that are found in my medical record file.

I hereby authorize the release of ANY & ALL records regarding mental health, psychiatric (other than psychotherapy notes, which must be requested under separate authorization), Chemical Dependency or HIV.

A photostatic copy hereof shall be as valid as the original.

I hereby authorize a FREE COPY of my medical records pursuant to KRS 422.317 be sent to the extent I have not already requested my one free copy.

The purpose of this authorization and request is to permit my attorney to obtain ALL medical information pertaining to my physical and mental condition(s). This authorization expires in three (3) years from the date of the signature. The aforementioned expiration date has not passed, as this matter is on-going. I hereby authorize The Whaley Law Firm, PSC. to speak to my healthcare providers privately or to take the testimony at

deposition and/or trial as may be requested.

I have the right to revoke this authorization in writing by providing a signed, written notice of revocation to the healthcare provider listed above and to The Whaley Law Firm, PSC. I understand that there are exceptions to the right to revoke. Among the exceptions to the right to revoke are that an individual may not revoke an authorization to the extent that the covered entity has taken action in reliance thereon. Medical Providers may not condition treatment or payment on whether the above-listed patient executes this authorization. The information disclosed pursuant to this authorization may be subject to re-disclosure and no longer protected by the privacy regulations promulgated pursuant to the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)

| had the great pleasure of seeing your patient, Daniel W Volk, at Norton Healthcare's multidisciplinary Brain Tumor Center.

In summary, Mr. Volk has a right parietal meningioma with small amount of edema. He has seen Dr. Spalding of radiation oncology and he would like to proceed craniotomy.

My detailed note including history, physical and recommendations is attached below.

If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to call me at my office at [PHONE-1247].

LD 4E—

David A. Sun M.D., Ph.D. Neurosurgeon

Co-Director, Brain Tumor Center Norton Neuroscience Institute

4950 Norton Healthcare Blvd, Suite 205 Louisville, KY 40241

[PHONE-988]

Clinical Notes (continued)

Treatment options at this time include: Continued surveillance.

Craniotomy for the purpose of diagnosis and maximal safe resection of the lesion. The potential benefits and risks of craniotomy were discussed at length. Risks include, but are not limited to, death, coma, paralysis, stroke, hemorrhage, damage to the senses, memory, cognition, language ability, need for tracheostomy or gastrostomy, meningitis, wound infection requiring bone flap removal, persistent pain, CSF leak, poor cosmetic result, and other risks that cannot be disclosed at this time due to their unforeseen nature. In addition, we discussed the fact that because of the nature of brain tumors, a degree of tumor tissue is most often left behind. Further treatment options, if needed, will be dictated by the tissue diagnosis.

Radiation therapy.

At this time, | recommend craniotomy for the purpose of diagnosis and maximal safe resection of the lesion. We will use a right parietal approach. All questions were answered to the best of my ability, and | believe that the patient understands. | agree with this plan, and we will schedule the procedure at his earliest convenience..

Thank you for allowing me to participate in the care of your patient. Please contact me with any questions or concerns.

Electronically signed by Sun, David A., MD at 4/29/2022 12:43 PM

MRI brain with contrast, brain laboratory protocol,

[DOB-993] at 0654 hours

HISTORY: Right parietal extra-axial mass.

TECHNIQUE: Multiple thin axial T2 and post enhanced axial T1-weighted images were obtained of the brain on the 3 Tesla magnet. FLAIR imaging was performed.

COMPARISON: MRI of the brain performed on January 7, 2022.

FINDINGS: There has been no interval change in the right posterior parafalcine meningioma. This measures 2.1 x 2.1 cm. This was 2.0 x 2.0 cm. Minimal surrounding edema is demonstrated.

There is no evidence of hydrocephalus. The ventricles are normal size and shape. Flow is seen within the vertebral basilar and carotid arteries. There is mild-to-moderate bilateral maxillary mucosal thickening.

Scattered opacification is noted in the ethmoid air cells. Mild-to-moderate mucosal thickening is seen in the frontal and sphenoid sinuses. No mastoid or middle ear effusion is demonstrated.

Injection Instructions

If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your doctor

After-Hours Instructions For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

MEDICAL RECORDS

To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or

complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org

Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.

Norton eCare Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.

Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.

IN NORTON

NEUROSCIENCE INSTITUTE

5/2/2022 Hello Mr. Volk:

Below is the information regarding your upcoming surgery with Dr. Sun. Attached are some instructions for your preparation for your surgery. Please call our office at [PHONE-1191] or reply to this message if you have any questions or concerns regarding the information below.

** **PLEASE REVIEW PRIOR TO SURGERY*™ **

We have you scheduled for a MRI at Norton Brownsboro Hospital, 4960 Norton Healthcare Blvd on 06.06.2022 arrive at 6:30 am for a 7:00 am MRI. These will be used for your surgical planning, so it is important that they get done. Below are the following instructions for both of these scans. If you have had any insurance changes since your last office visit please contact the office to update your information.

MRI Patients Instructions:

For patient safety, they will be changed into a hospital gown or scrubs during the MRI procedure. Jewelry, including body piercings must be removed prior to the exam. Do not wear heavy eye make-up, magnetic lash extensions, wigs/hairpieces or hairpins for your exam. Patients with any type of surgical implant, bring implant card to appointment or provide to MRI department prior to the date of service.

Your surgeon is: Dr. Sun

You will be contacted by the pre admission testing department via phone (502-446-XXXxX) prior to your surgery date to go over your medical information and schedule your lab tests. You must be sure to answer the phone call or call them back. They will not call back after 3 attempts and the surgery will be cancelled. There is no specific day or time the call will be at this time.

You are required to have a COVID-19 swab or to have the two vaccines from Moderna/Pzifer or the one shot from Johnson & Johnson.

Your surgery has been scheduled at Norton Brownsboro Hospital (4960 Norton Healthcare Blvd. Louisville,

KY 40241) on 06.21.2022, you will need to register at 5:30 am.

IN NORTON NORTON PHYSICIANS Volk, Daniel W

HEALTHCARE SERVICES MRN: EP01968571, DOB: 2/22/1964, Sex: M Real people. Remarkable care 1930 Bishop Ln Visit date: 5/2/2022 Peeew STE 1200

Louisville KY 40218-1929 [DOB-1190] - Patient Message in Norton Neuroscience Institute (continued)

Messages (continued) Because surgery times constantly change, even at the last minute, we will call you the business day

before surgery to confirm times. Please be aware you may be asked to arrive as early as 5:00AM. Upon arrival to the hospital, your surgery time may have changed due to circumstances beyond our control.

Your procedure may start later than the originally scheduled time.

Your post op appointment is scheduled on 07.08.2022 @ 9:00 am at Norton Brownsboro. Pre-Admission Testing (PAT) and Surgery Instructions:

You will be contacted by the pre admission testing department via phone (502-446-XXXxX) prior to your surgery date to go over your medical information and schedule your lab tests. You must be sure to answer the phone call or call them back. They will not call back after 3 attempts and the surgery will be cancelled. There is no specific day or time the call will be at this time. During the call a Pre-admission Testing appointment will be made for you for 2-3 weeks prior to your surgery. Please keep in mind Pre-admission Testing can take 30 mins - 2 hours. If you reschedule your PAT for any reason closer to surgery and you need any type of clearance your surgery may be rescheduled to the next available date.

At PAT appointments patients will meet with an RN and give a medical history, discuss their medications - (bring medicine bottles or a complete list to include drug name, dose and frequency), have lab work, Chest x-ray and EKG if needed. Surgical teaching/explanation of what to expect before, during and after surgery will also be done during this appointment.

If our office or PAT advised you to seek medical, cardiac and/or other clearance, you are responsible for making that appointment with your physician for the clearance to be done. The hospital only faxes your results to your physician. You must have this faxed to our office prior to your surgery at [PHONE-1189]. Call me with your appointment date so | can watch for the clearance. If clearances cannot be obtained prior to surgery we will move your surgery date.

Where to go:

The date of your PAT appointment:

Norton Brownsboro Hospital. You will check in at registration in the lobby. You do not need to fast for this appointment and should drink plenty of water.

The date of Surgery:

Norton Brownsboro Hospital MAIN OR: When you arrive for surgery, enter the hospital through the main entrance and check in at registration in the lobby. If you are unable to arrive on time, it's important you call [PHONE-1188]. If you are asked to arrive prior to 5:30AM, you will need to enter through the ER.

Because surgery times constantly change, even at the last minute, we will call you the business day before surgery to confirm times. Please be aware you may be asked to arrive as early as 5:00AM. Upon arrival to the hospital, your surgery time may have changed due to circumstances beyond our control. Your procedure may start later than the originally scheduled time.

7 Days Prior to Surgery:

Stop Anti-inflammatory medications, NSAIDS (examples: Ibuprofen, Aleve, Advil, Celebrex, Vioxx). Stop taking Aspirin or any Aspirin containing products.

Generated on 12/6/23 6:10 AM Page 52




IN NORTON NORTON PHYSICIANS Volk, Daniel W

HEALTHCARE SERVICES MRN: EP01968571, DOB: 2/22/1964, Sex: M Real people. Remarkable care 1930 Bishop Ln Visit date: 5/2/2022 Peeew STE 1200

Louisville KY 40218-1929 [DOB-1047] - Patient Message in Norton Neuroscience Institute (continued)

Messages (continued)

Stop Plavix, Coumadin or any other blood thinners as approved by the prescribing physician. You may continue taking your pain medication if it contains acetaminophen (i.e. Norco) but as little as possible. You CAN take Extra Strength Tylenol if needed.

5 Days Prior to Surgery:

A prescription for Bactroban ointment will be sent to your pharmacy. Start this medication 5 days prior to surgery and follow the instructions on the prescription.

Night Before Surgery:

NOTHING TO EAT OR DRINK AFTER MIDNIGHT. Failure to follow these instructions can lead to an increased risk of lung complications and may result in a delay or cancellation of your procedure. If you have any questions, contact your institution for further clarification.

Take a full bath/shower. Shower daily with chlorhexidine wash (CHG). Use this soap below the head, and do not get it in your eyes, ears, nose, mouth, or genitals. This is a strong soap that decreases the bacterial load on your skin and is important for preventing surgical infection. You will be given this soap at PAT clinic. If you do not receive it or need more, you may purchase it at Walgreens or CVS.

Day of Surgery:

Shower daily with chlorhexidine wash (CHG). Use this soap below the head, and do not get it in your eyes, ears, nose, mouth, or genitals.

This is a strong soap that decreases the bacterial load on your skin and is important for preventing surgical infection. You will be given this soap at PAT clinic. If you do not receive it or need more, you may purchase it at Walgreens or CVS.

If you have dentures, make sure you give them to a family member or to someone who can secure them before you enter the procedure.

Do not wear make-up, lotions, powder, perfume or after shave the day of surgery. You may brush your teeth and wear deodorant.

Wear comfortable, loose fitting clothes to the hospital.

Leave all of your jewelry and valuables at home.

Wear your glasses or bring your contact case and solutions required to safely. remove your lenses; contact lenses are not permitted to be worn during surgery

If your surgery is scheduled as an outpatient procedure, make arrangements for a responsible adult to take you home after your procedure. You will not be allowed to leave alone or drive yourself home. It is suggested that you have someone with you the first 24 hours after surgery.

Please bring your insurance card, photo ID. Please note you may be responsible for hospital payment day of

surgery. Medications the Day of Surgery:

Patients should take their cardiac, antihypertensives, Beta-blocker (including combination drug with Beta- blocker), respiratory, gastric, thyroid and anticonvulsant medications on the morning of surgery with a tiny sip of water. (The nurses in PAT will give you a list of the medications you can take the morning of surgery).

Patients may use respiratory inhaler morning of surgery and bring inhaler to the hospital.

Do not take blood pressure medications that contain a diuretic unless the medication also contains a beta- blocker.

All patients are to bring their CPAP machine to be used during surgery.

Insurance Information:

We have contacted your insurance company for prior authorization only, this may still be pending at the time of scheduling but we do not anticipate any problems with this. It is your responsibility to check on your benefits. The office cannot give pricing or copay information. If your insurance changes and you do not notify the office prior to the day of surgery, your surgery may be rescheduled or denied by the insurance for no authorization. After Your Surgery:

You will receive post op instructions prior to leaving the hospital after surgery.

If you experience any problems or have questions call our office's main number [PHONE-1185] and follow the prompts to your physician's medical assistant. You may need to leave a message and a Clinical Staff or the Physician will return your call. Any calls left after 3:00 PM may not be returned until the next business day.

If you have problems after 4:30PM, you will need to call the main number and the Answering Service will page a physician.

6/20/2022 11:36 AM by Daniel W Volk

Good Afternoon Mr. Volk,

In regards to surgery tomorrow with Dr. Sun, your surgery is scheduled to begin at 8:00 am, with an arrival time of 5:30 am at the NBH main registration desk.

Please remember that you are to have NOTHING TO EAT OR DRINK AFTER MIDNIGHT, THE NIGHT BEFORE YOUR SURGERY. Failure to follow these instructions can lead to an increased risk of lung complications and may result in a delay or cancellation of your procedure. If you have any questions, contact your institution for further clarification.

Thank you,

[DOB-1153] - MRV Brain in AUD MRI (continued) Imaging (continued)

Loss of flow-related signal within the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor with surrounding collateral vessel formation The remainder the superior sagittal sinus demonstrates patent flow related signal

Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portion of the right transverse sinus is present. The left transverse sinus demonstrates normal flow-related signal The sigmoid sinuses. internal cerebral veins, vein of Galen, and straight sinus demonstrate

normal flow related signal

IMPRESSION

Focal absence of flow related signal involving the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor demonstrated on the MRI brain from the same day. Small surrounding collateral vessel formation. Constellation of findings is suspicious for local tumor invasion/thrombosis. The remainder of the superior sagittal sinus maintains flow related signal. Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portions of the right transverse sinus which may reflect slow flow associated with a nondominant sinus although a component of chronic dural venous sinus thrombosis/stenosis not entirely excluded There is no

clear evidence for acute durai venous sinus thrombosis based on the MRV and corresponding brain MRI. No parenchymal infarcts

Brain mass or lesion, follow-up incidental finding demonstrated during workup following MVC COMPARISON None

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multi-weighted MR imaging of the brain was performed before and after the intravenous administration of 20 mL of Dotarem contrast

FINDINGS

Enhancing posterior right parafalcine extra-axial lesion measures 2.0 x 2 0 cm (series 16, image 99) axial dimension and 2 3 cm craniocaudal dimension (15-30) The enhancement is fairly homogeneous with the exception of a small region of central

Generated on 1/28/22 2:10 PM Page 10




AN NORTON NORTON AUDUBON Volk, Daniel W

HEALTHCARE HOSPITAL MRN: EP01968571, DOB: 2/22/1964, Sex: M 1 Audubon Plaza Drive Acct #: 213030549 Real people. Remarkable care . Louisville KY 40217-1318 Adm: 1/7/2022, D/C: 1/7/2022

[DOB-1138] - MRI BRAIN W/WO in AUD MRI (continued)

Imaging (continued)

nonenhancement which could be related to internal cystic change Mild mass effect and parenchymal edema affects the posterior right parietal lobe. Enhancing tumor likely invades the superior sagittal sinus at this level and there is mild enhancement and

thickening along the left aspect of the falx at this level which also is suspicious for additional tumor involvement. No definite calvarial invasion. There are no additional enhancing intracranial lesions

There is no restricted diffusion. There is no hemorrhage, hydrocephalus or midline shift. The major intracranial arterial flow voids appear normal. There is partial opacification of the ethmoid air cells. There is a mucous retention cyst in left maxillary sinus. There are no sinus fluid levels. Partially empty CSF filled sella

IMPRESSION:

Enhancing posterior right parafalcine extra-axial lesion causes mild locai mass effect and parenchymal edema on the adjacent posterior right parietal lobe There is possible local invasion of the superior sagittal sinus at this level by enhancing tumor

The imaging findings statistically most likely represent a meningioma although other neoplasms could have a similar appearance. If there is any older neuroimaging available, this would be helpful to confirm stability of these findings

MR VENOGRAPHY BRAIN WITHOUT CONTRAST

HISTORY. Incidental brain lesion demonstrated on recent outside MRI which is not available for comparison and concerning for meningioma

COMPARISON None

TECHNIQUE. 3-D phase contrast magnetic resonance venography was performed of the brain including 3-D MIP reformatted images

FINDINGS

Loss of flow-related signal within the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor with surrounding collateral vessel formation The remainder the superior sagittal sinus demonstrates patent flow related signal.

Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portion of the right transverse sinus Is present. The left transverse sinus demonstrates normal flow-related signal. The sigmoid sinuses. internal cerebral veins. vein of Galen, and straight sinus demonstrate

normal flow related signal

IMPRESSION Focal absence of flow related signal involving the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor

demonstrated on the MRI brain from the same day. Small surrounding collateral vessel formation. Constellation of findings is suspicious for local tumor invasion/thrombosis The remainder of the superior sagittal sinus maintains flow related signal

Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portions of the right transverse sinus which may reflect slow flow associated with a nondominant sinus although a component of chronic dural venous sinus thrombosis/stenosis not entirely excluded There is no

clear evidence for acute dural venous sinus thrombosis based on the MRV and corresponding brain MRI. No parenchymal infarcts

Narrative:

REVIEWING YOUR TEST RESULTS IN MYNORTONCHART IS NOT A SUBSTITUTE FOR DISCUSSING THOSE RESULTS WITH YOUR HEALTH CARE PROVIDER.

PLEASE CONTACT YOUR PROVIDER VIA MYNORTONCHART TO DISCUSS ANY QUESTIONS OR CONCERNS YOU MAY HAVE REGARDING THESE TEST RESULTS.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FACILITY: NORTON AUDUBON HOSPITAL UNIT/AGE/GENDER: D.MRI OP AGE:57 Y SEX:M PATIENT NAME/DOB: VOLK, DANIEL [DOB-1]

UNIT NUMBER: EP01968571

ACCOUNT NUMBER: 10122976623

ACCESSION NUMBER: EPD22MRI7216

MR VENOGRAPHY BRAIN WITHOUT CONTRAST

HISTORY: Incidental brain lesion demonstrated on recent outside MRI which is not available for comparison and concerning for meningioma

COMPARISON: None

TECHNIQUE: 3-D phase contrast magnetic resonance venography was performed of the brain including 3-D MIP reformatted images.

FINDINGS:

Loss of flow-related signal within the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor with surrounding collateral vessel formation. The remainder the superior sagittal sinus demonstrates patent flow related signal.

Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portion of the right transverse sinus is present. The left transverse sinus demonstrates normal flow-related signal. The sigmoid sinuses, internal cerebral veins, vein of Galen, and straight sinus demonstrate

normal flow related signal.

IMPRESSION:

Focal absence of flow related signal involving the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor demonstrated on the MRI brain from the same day. Small surrounding collateral vessel formation. Constellation of findings is suspicious for local tumor invasion/thrombosis. The remainder of the superior sagittal sinus maintains flow related signal. Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portions of the right transverse sinus which may reflect slow flow associated with a nondominant sinus although a component of chronic dural venous sinus thrombosis/stenosis not entirely excluded. There is no clear evidence for acute dural venous sinus thrombosis based on the MRV and corresponding brain MRI. No parenchymal infarcts.

Dictated by: Michael Hogan, M.D.

Images and Report reviewed and interpreted by: Michael Hogan, M.D.

Narrative:

REVIEWING YOUR TEST RESULTS IN MYNORTONCHART IS NOT A SUBSTITUTE FOR DISCUSSING THOSE RESULTS WITH YOUR HEALTH CARE PROVIDER.

PLEASE CONTACT YOUR PROVIDER VIA MYNORTONCHART TO DISCUSS ANY QUESTIONS OR CONCERNS YOU MAY HAVE REGARDING THESE TEST RESULTS.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FACILITY: NORTON AUDUBON HOSPITAL UNIT/AGE/GENDER: D.MRI OP AGE:57 Y SEX:M PATIENT NAME/DOB: VOLK, DANIEL [DOB-1]

UNIT NUMBER: EP01968571

ACCOUNT NUMBER: 10122976621

ACCESSION NUMBER: EPD22MRI7222

MRI BRAIN WITHOUT AND WITH CONTRAST, 1/7/2022 7:18 AM HISTORY: Brain mass or lesion, follow-up, incidental finding demonstrated during workup following MVC COMPARISON: None

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multi-weighted MR imaging of the brain was performed before and after the intravenous administration of 20 mL of Dotarem contrast.

FINDINGS:

Enhancing posterior right parafalcine extra-axial lesion measures 2.0 x 2.0 cm (series 16, image 99) axial dimension and 2.3 cm craniocaudal dimension (15-30). The enhancement is fairly homogeneous with the exception of a small region of central nonenhancement which could be related to internal cystic change. Mild mass effect and parenchymal edema affects the posterior right parietal lobe. Enhancing tumor likely invades the superior sagittal sinus at this level and there is mild enhancement and

thickening along the left aspect of the falx at this level which also is suspicious for additional tumor involvement. No definite calvarial invasion. There are no additional enhancing intracranial lesions.

There is no restricted diffusion. There is no hemorrhage, hydrocephalus or midline shift. The major intracranial arterial flow voids appear normal. There is partial opacification of the ethmoid air cells. There is a mucous retention cyst in left

maxillary sinus. There are no sinus fluid levels. Partially empty CSF filled sella.

IMPRESSION:

Enhancing posterior right parafalcine extra-axial lesion causes mild local mass effect and parenchymal edema on the adjacent posterior right parietal lobe. There is possible local invasion of the superior sagittal sinus at this level by enhancing tumor.

The imaging findings statistically most likely represent a meningioma although other neoplasms could have a similar appearance. If there is any older neuroimaging available, this would be helpful to confirm stability of these findings.

MR VENOGRAPHY BRAIN WITHOUT CONTRAST

HISTORY: Incidental brain lesion demonstrated on recent outside MRI which is not available for comparison and concerning for meningioma

COMPARISON: None

Generated on 1/30/24 10:55 AM Page 19




IN NORTON NORTON AUDUBON Volk, Daniel W

HEALTHCARE HOSPITAL MRN: EP01968571, DOB: 2/22/1964, Sex: M neal people: Remarkable care 1 Audubon Plaza Drive Acct #: 213030549 , . Louisville KY 40217-1318 Adm: 1/7/2022, D/C: 1/7/2022

[DOB-1172] - MRI BRAIN W/WO in AUD MRI (continued)

Imaging (continued)

TECHNIQUE: 3-D phase contrast magnetic resonance venography was performed of the brain including 3-D MIP reformatted images. FINDINGS:

Loss of flow-related signal within the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor with surrounding collateral vessel formation. The remainder the superior sagittal sinus demonstrates patent flow related signal.

Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portion of the right transverse sinus is present. The left transverse sinus demonstrates normal flow-related signal. The sigmoid sinuses, internal cerebral veins, vein of Galen, and straight sinus demonstrate

normal flow related signal.

IMPRESSION:

Focal absence of flow related signal involving the superior sagittal sinus at the level of the enhancing parafalcine tumor demonstrated on the MRI brain from the same day. Small surrounding collateral vessel formation. Constellation of findings is suspicious for local tumor invasion/thrombosis. The remainder of the superior sagittal sinus maintains flow related signal. Attenuated appearance of the mid and distal portions of the right transverse sinus which may reflect slow flow associated with a nondominant sinus although a component of chronic dural venous sinus thrombosis/stenosis not entirely excluded. There is no clear evidence for acute dural venous sinus thrombosis based on the MRV and corresponding brain MRI. No parenchymal infarcts.

Dictated by: Michael Hogan, M.D.

Images and Report reviewed and interpreted by: Michael Hogan, M.D.

(Authorization to Release Protected Health Care Information)

To: Cressman Neurological Rehabilitation 4915 Norton Healthcare Blvd. Louisville, KY 40241

Dear Medical Records Clerk:

I have retained The Whaley Law Firm, PSC to represent me in matters related to an injury sustained due to a third parties’ negligence. Please forward a copy of any & all records not previously provided to The Whaley Law Firm upon request.

Pursuant to the Health Insurance Portability & Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy Regulations, 45 CFR 164.508, the provider listed above is hereby authorized to release to The Whaley Law Firm, PSC or any of their representatives, all medical records, including but not limited to: Office Notes, History, Physical, Consultation Notes, Discharge Summaries, Orders, Progress Notes, Laboratory Results, Nurses’ Notes, Emergency Room Records, Operative Reports, In-Patient Records and films of X-Rays, MRIs, CT Scans, or PET Scans, Pharmacy and Drug Records, Medical Bills and Health Insurance Information, Medicaid/Medicare Records, concerning ANY medical treatment that I have received from you and/or at your facility, as well as ALL such records which you keep in the regular course of business that are found in my medical record file.

I hereby authorize the release of ANY & ALL records regarding mental health, psychiatric (other than psychotherapy notes, which must be requested under separate authorization), Chemical Dependency or HIV.

A photostatic copy hereof shall be as valid as the original.

Thereby authorize a FREE COPY of my medical records pursuant to KRS 422.317 be sent to the extent I have not already requested my one free copy.

The purpose of this authorization and request is to permit my attorney to obtain ALL medical information pertaining to my physical and mental condition(s). This authorization expires in three (3) years from the date of the signature. The aforementioned expiration date has not passed, as this matter is on-going. I hereby authorize The Whaley Law Firm, PSC. to speak to my healthcare providers privately or to take the testimony at deposition and/or trial as may be requested.

T have the right to revoke this authorization in writing by providing a signed, written notice of revocation to the healthcare provider listed above and to The Whaley Law Firm, PSC. I understand that there are exceptions to the right to revoke. Among the exceptions to the right to revoke are that an individual may not revoke an authorization to the extent that the covered entity has taken action in reliance thereon, Medical Providers may not condition treatment or payment on whether the above-listed patient executes this authorization. The information disclosed pursuant to this authorization may be subject to re-disclosure and no longer protected by the privacy regulations promulgated pursuant to the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)

S/p craniotomy with Dr. Sun to remove meningioma on 6/21/22

Initially had weakness on L side ; feels like L leg is stronger from walking more

Had car accident prior to surgery and from imaging found tumor

From accident had rotator cuff tear and labral tear- saw PT afterwards which was helpful trying to avoid surgery

Natalie Vance, PT

Pt pleased with his progress. Recommended to resume exercise based on post op guidelines he received and to monitor his vitals throughout.
