Right____ Left____ interscalene Supraclavicular Infraclavicular Axillary Wrist Digital Femoral Saphenous Popliteal Sciatic Ankle — Eye

Description: Temporary loss of feeling and/or movement of a limb Technique: Local anesthetic injected via a needle with nerve twitch monitor and/ or ultrasound guidance providing loss of sensation to the limb or area of the operation Risks: Infection; convulsions; weakness; persistent numbness, residual pain; injury to blood vessels (eye block-loss of vision); inability to breathe on your own which may require breathing assistance up to and including placement of a tube into the windpipe; collapsed lung; hematoma; temporary paralysis of the diaphragm; chronic pain; and medical necessity to convert to general anesthesia; nerve damage

RISKS AND HAZARDS ASSOCIATED WITH INVASIVE MONITORS:

Invasive monitors are devices that enter the body to aid the physician in making decisions regarding the patient’s vital functions that cannot be obtained by non-invasive monitors. Certain risks involved in using invasive monitors include: injuries to blood vessels and/or the heart; infection; puncture of lung; collapse of the lung; injury to nerves; stroke; loss of digits; anxiety and/or discomfort.

ADDITIONAL RISKS TO SPECIFIC SURGERIES:

While complications are rare, different surgeries carry special risks. For example, spinal surgery has specific risks which you and your surgeon may have already discussed, such as the risk of blindness after surgery. Blindness can affect one or both eyes, may be partial or complete, temporary or rarely permanent. This risk is related to prone (belly down) position, as well as multiple other factors. Some risks are not necessarily dependent on the surgery or anesthesia alone. Your age, weight, and other health conditions may also affect your risk. Please do not hesitate to ask your anesthesiologist any questions you may have regarding specific risks to your surgery. Additional risks discussed:

MEDICATION GUIDE CLARISCAN (kla-ri'-skan) (gadoterate meglumine) Injection for intravenous use

What is Clariscan?

e . Clariscan is a prescription medicine called a gadolinium- -based contrast agent (GBCA). Clariscan, like other GBCAs, is injected into your vein and used with a magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanner.

e — AnMRI exam with a GBCA, including Clariscan, helps your doctor to see problems better than an MRI exam without a GBCA.

. Your doctor has Feviewed your medical records and has determined that you would benefit from using a GBCA with your MRI exam.

Subjective:

S/p craniotomy with Dr. Sun to remove meningioma on 6/21/22

Initially had weakness on L side ; feels like L leg is stronger from walking more

L arm is a little weaker, occasional numbness/tingling intermittently and occasional action tremor when holding things in L hand

Had car accident prior to surgery and from imaging found tumor

From accident had rotator cuff tear and labral tear- saw PT afterwards which was helpful trying to avoid surgery

Patient has returned to baseline

Pt is aa 58 y/o male who presents to Cressman s/p craniotomy to remove R parietal meningioma. Pt with mild residual weakness of LUE that he feels like is continually improving. Occasional paraesthesias in LUE and slight action tremor noted on eval today. PT educated on monitoring this and techniques to improve LUE coordination. To f/u with Dr. Sun if tremor worsens. Overall pt is doing excellent and appears to have returned to his baseline function. Extensive testing done today to evaluate balance, strength and endurance with pt scoring within normal range for all outcomes. No functional deficits noted on evaluation today. Pt pleased with his progress. Recommended to resume exercise based on post op guidelines he received and to monitor his vitals throughout. Pt demonstrated good understanding and recommended to f/u if any further functional needs

I. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, AND MEDICAL TREATMENT:

I voluntarily consent to care that involves routine diagnostic tests, procedures, and medical treatment as prescribed by my physician and performed by the facility. No guarantee has been given by anyone as to the results that may be obtained. This consent includes testing for Hepatitis, Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) or any other blood-borne infectious diseases, if ordered for diagnostic purposes or due to an occupational exposure. I consent to photographing, videotaping, video monitoring and audio devices for medical, scientific, safety or education purposes as well as for the purposes of identification. I understand that these will be stored in a secure manner that will protect my privacy. Images that identify me will only be released when authorized by me or as needed to provide for my continued medical treatment or identification.

Il. OTHER PRACTITIONERS AND SUPPLIERS OF SERVICES:

I understand and acknowledge that I may require the services of physicians, groups of physicians or other practitioners (such as nurses and physician assistants) who are not employees of the facility, including, but not limited to, anesthesiologists, radiologists, emergency department physicians, certified registered nurse anesthetists, and other practitioners. I also understand that residents, fellows, medical students, and students of various health care fields may be involved in my care and that they are not employees of the facility. I agree that the signing of this document does not in any way imply that Norton Healthcare is responsible for or assumes any liability for the activities of any such physicians or practitioners who are not its employees. When a physician, other practitioner or supplier of services bills me directly, I agree to pay the bill. I agree that the provisions regarding the release of information and assignment of benefits set out in paragraphs III, V, and VI below shall apply not only to the facility but also to such physicians, practitioners and suppliers of services and their claims for payment.

Ill. RELEASE OF INFORMATION:

I authorize the facility to notify my referring and treating physician(s), of my admission to the facility and to release any information about me as requested by such physician(s). I authorize the facility and any other holder of medical or other information about me including medical information concerning drug related conditions, alcoholism, psychological conditions, psychiatric conditions, and/or blood-borne infectious diseases, to release to the Social Security Administration, its intermediaries and its carriers, State agencies, the review organization employed by my employer, or the employer of the insured member of my family, or any private third-party payor, as appropriate, any information required for the completion of any claim for benefits arising out of services rendered to me either on an inpatient or outpatient basis. I further authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA). I authorize the release of my HIV test results to healthcare personnel in the event of an occupational exposure. If my injury is work-related, I authorize the facility and any other holder of medical or other information about me to release from my medical record any information as requested by the company and/or its workers compensation administrator as indicated below:

THE FACILITY IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR LOSS OF PERSONAL ARTICLES:

I understand that the facility maintains a safe for the safekeeping of money and valuables and the facility shall not be liable for the loss of or damage to

any money, jewelry, documents, or any other items of personal property, unless deposited in the facility safe for safekeeping. Furthermore, I accept full

responsibility for items of personal property kept in my possession.

V. ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:

I agree to the assignment of all third-party benefits to the facility and agree to pay the facility for all charges not covered by third-party payors. In the

case of outpatient services I agree that this document shall remain in full force and effect until specifically revoked by me in writing. Any sums not paid

by a third-party payor are my obligation.

VI. RELEASE OF INSURANCE INFORMATION TO FACILITY:

I agree to the release by my insurance carrier to the facility and the facility's billing service of any eligibility and utilization data concerning my insurance

coverage which the facility and/or its billing supplier may require. I authorize the facility to file an appeal of a payment denial and/or request a hearing

regarding any denial of payment for treatment provided to me. I further authorize the facility to present proof, utilize facility records and my records

and documentation for appeals and hearings.

VII. PAYMENT OF FACILITY BILLS WHEN DUE:

I am liable for the payment of the account of the facility and I am obligated to pay the account of the facility, in accordance with the regular rates and

terms of the facility. If my injury is work-related, I am further obligated to pay the account of the facility in the event my employer or its third-party

administrator denies payment for workers compensation. It is understood that the uninsured portion of bills are payable at the end of each week and in full

upon discharge from the facility. I understand that after reasonable notice, delinquent accounts may be turned over to a collection agency and/or an

attorney for collection. Should it be necessary to place this account in the hands of an attorney for collection, I agree to pay the costs of collection,

including any court costs and a reasonable fee to the attorney employed by the facility to collect the account.

I agree that the facility, its employees, agents, attorneys and collection agencies, may call or text the cell number I provide for any reason, including but

not limited to inquiring about the status and collection of my account. I also expressly authorize that each of them may use any mechanism, including but

not limited to automatic dialing, artificial and prerecorded voice systems.

REVIEWING YOUR TEST RESULTS IN MYNORTONCHART IS NOT A SUBSTITUTE FOR DISCUSSING THOSE RESULTS WITH YOUR HEALTH CARE PROVIDER.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FACILITY: NORTON BROWNSBORO HOSPITAL

UNIT/AGE/GENDER: J.MRIHOS OP AGE:58 Y SEX:M

PATIENT NAME/DOB: VOLK, DANIEL, W [DOB-1]

UNIT NUMBER: EP01968571

ACCOUNT NUMBER: 10144180892 ACCESSION NUMBER: EPJ23MRI50697

CLINICAL HISTORY: Complex patient

brain mass

COMPARISON: MRI brain June 22, 2022

TECHNIQUE: Sagittal T1; axial T1, T2, FLAIR, gradient echo, diffusion. Axial, sagittal, and coronal T1 postcontrast. FINDINGS:

Post surgical change of midline posterior parietal craniotomy for resection of right parietal meningioma. No new enhancement at the resection site. Decreased conspicuity of FLAIR hyperintense signal within the right parietal lobe at the resection site. There is no restricted diffusion to suggest acute ischemia.

The ventricles and basilar cisterns are normal in size and configuration for the patients age.

There is no evidence of mass effect or shift of the midline structures.

Posterior fossa structures including the cerebellum and brainstem are unremarkable.

Limited imaging of the orbits demonstrates no orbital abnormality.

There are normal flow voids in the arteries at the level of the Circle of Willis.

Retention cyst in the inferior maxillary sinuses. Mastoid air cells are clear.

IMPRESSION: Stable post surgical changes of resection of right parietal meningioma. No findings to suggest lesion recurrence.

Dictated by: Melissa Smith, M.D. Images and Report reviewed and interpreted by: Melissa Smith, M.D.

I. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, AND MEDICAL TREATMENT:

I voluntarily consent to care that involves routine diagnostic tests, procedures, and medical treatment as prescribed by my physician and performed by the facility. No guarantee has been given by anyone as to the results that may be obtained. This consent includes testing for Hepatitis, Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) or any other blood-borne infectious diseases, if ordered for diagnostic purposes or due to an occupational exposure. I consent to photographing, videotaping, video monitoring and audio devices for medical, scientific, safety or education purposes as well as for the purposes of identification. I understand that these will be stored in a secure manner that will protect my privacy. Images that identify me will only be released when authorized by me or as needed to provide for my continued medical treatment or identification.

Il. OTHER PRACTITIONERS AND SUPPLIERS OF SERVICES:

I understand and acknowledge that I may require the services of physicians, groups of physicians or other practitioners (such as nurses and physician assistants) who are not employees of the facility, including, but not limited to, anesthesiologists, radiologists, emergency department physicians, certified registered nurse anesthetists, and other practitioners. I also understand that residents, fellows, medical students, and students of various health care fields may be involved in my care and that they are not employees of the facility. I agree that the signing of this document does not in any way imply that Norton Healthcare is responsible for or assumes any liability for the activities of any such physicians or practitioners who are not its employees. When a physician, other practitioner or supplier of services bills me directly, I agree to pay the bill. I agree that the provisions regarding the release of information and assignment of benefits set out in paragraphs III, V, and VI below shall apply not only to the facility but also to such physicians, practitioners and suppliers of services and their claims for payment.

Ill. RELEASE OF INFORMATION:

I authorize the facility to notify my referring and treating physician(s), of my admission to the facility and to release any information about me as requested by such physician(s). I authorize the facility and any other holder of medical or other information about me including medical information concerning drug related conditions, alcoholism, psychological conditions, psychiatric conditions, and/or blood-borne infectious diseases, to release to the Social Security Administration, its intermediaries and its carriers, State agencies, the review organization employed by my employer, or the employer of the insured member of my family, or any private third-party payor, as appropriate, any information required for the completion of any claim for benefits arising out of services rendered to me either on an inpatient or outpatient basis. I further authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA). I authorize the release of my HIV test results to healthcare personnel in the event of an occupational exposure. MEDICATION GUIDE CLARISCAN (kla-ri’-skan) (gadoterate meglumine) Injection for intravenous use

What is Clariscan?

Clariscan is a prescription medicine called a gadolinium-based contrast agent (GBCA). Clariscan, like other GBCAs, is injected into your vein and used with a magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanner.

An MRI exam with a GBCA, including Clariscan, helps your doctor to see problems better than an MRI exam without a GBCA.

Your doctor has reviewed your medical records and has determined that you would benefit from using a GBCA with your MRI exam.

What is the most important information | should know about Clariscan?

° °

Clariscan contains a metal called gadolinium. Small amounts of gadolinium can stay in your body including the brain, bones, skin and other parts of your body for a long time (several months to years). :

It is not known how gadolinium may affect you, but so far, studies have not found harmful effects in patients with normal kidneys.

Rarely patients have reported pains, tiredness, and skin, muscle or bone ailments for a long time, but these symptoms have not been directly linked to gadolinium.

There are different GBCAs that can be used for your MRI exam. The amount of gadolinium that stays in the body is different for different gadolinium medicines. Gadolinium stays in the body more after Omniscan or Optimark than after Eovist, Magnevist or MultiHance. Gadolinium stays in the body the least after Clariscan, Dotarem, Gadavist or ProHance.

People who get many doses of gadolinium medicines, women who are pregnant and young children may be at increased risk from gadolinium staying in the body.

Some people with kidney problems who get gadolinium medicines can develop a condition with severe thickening of the skin, muscles and other organs in the body (nephrogenic systemic fibrosis). Your healthcare provider should screen you to see how well your kidneys are working before you receive Clariscan.

Do not receive Clariscan if you have had a severe allergic reaction to Clariscan.

Before receiving Clariscan, tell your healthcare provider about all your medical conditions, including if you:

Have had any MRI procedures in the past where you received a GBCA. Your healthcare provider may ask you for more information including the dates of these MRI procedures.

Are pregnant or plan to become pregnant. It is not known if Clariscan can harm your unborn baby. Talk to your healthcare provider about the possible risks to an unborn baby if a GBCA such as Clariscan is received during pregnancy.

Have kidney problems, diabetes, or high blood pressure.

Have had an allergic reaction to dyes (contrast agents) including GBCAs.

What are possible side effects of Clariscan?

See “What is the most important information | should know about Clariscan?" Allergic reactions. Clariscan can cause allergic reactions that can sometimes be serious. Your healthcare provider will monitor you closely for symptoms of an allergic reaction.

The most common side effects of Clariscan include: nausea, headache, pain, or cold feeling at the injection site, and rash.

These are not all the possible side effects of Clariscan. Call your doctor for medical advice about side effects. You may report side effects to FDA at 1-800-FDA-1088.

General information about the safe and effective uses of Clariscan. Medicines are sometimes prescribed for purposes other than those listed in a Medication Guide. You can ask your healthcare provider for information about Clariscan that is written for health professionals.

What are the ingredients in Clariscan? Active ingredient: gadoterate meglumine Inactive ingredients: DOTA, water for injection

Manufactured by: GE Healthcare AS Oslo, Norway

GE and the GE Monogram are trademarks of General Electric Company.

For more information, go to www.GEHealthcare.com or call 1-[PHONE-1959].

This Medication Guide has been approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. 08/2020

Pursuant to the Health Insurance Portability & Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy Regulations, 45 CFR 164.508, the provider listed above is hereby authorized to release to The Whaley Law Firm, PSC or any of their representatives, all medical records, including but not limited to: Office Notes, History, Physical, Consultation Notes, Discharge Summaries, Orders, Progress Notes, Laboratory Results, Nurses' Notes, Emergency Room Records, Operative Reports, In-Patient Records and films of X-Rays, MRIs, CT Scans, ar PET Scans, Pharmacy and Drug Records, Medical Bills and Health Insurance Information, Medicaid/Medicare Records, concerning ANY medical treatment that I have received from you and/or at your facility, as well as ALL such records which you keep in the regular course of business that are found in my medical record file.

I hereby authorize the release of ANY & ALL records regarding mental health, psychiatric (other than psychotherapy notes, which must be requested under separate authorization), Chemical Dependency or HIV.

A photostatic copy hereof shall be as valid as the original.

I hereby authorize a FREE COPY of my medical records pursuant to KRS 422.317 be sent to the extent I have not already requested my one free copy.

The purpose of this authorization and request is to permit my attorney to obtain ALL medical information pertaining to my physical and mental ~- «.—_condition(s)This authorization expires in three (3) years from the date of the signature. The aforementioned ed expiration date has not passed, : as

this matter is on-going. I hereby authorize The Whaley Law Firm, PSC. to speak to my healthcare providers privately or to take the testimony at deposition and/or trial as may be requested.

Thave the right to revoke this authorization in writing by providing a signed, written notice of revocation to the healthcare provider listed above and to The Whaley Law Firm, PSC. I understand that there are exceptions to the right to revoke. Among the exceptions to the right to revoke are that an individual may not revoke an authorization to the extent that the covered entity has taken action in reliance thereon. Medical Providers may not condition treatment or payment on whether the above-listed patient executes this authorization. The information disclosed pursuant to this authorization may be subject to re-disclosure and no longer protected by the privacy regulations promulgated pursuant fo the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)

Thank you for your request, we are happy to serve you.

Please be advised that Ciox Health has been contracted by the health facility’s centralized billing office which you are requesting records from.

We recognize that your request has asked for both medical records and billing records.

We have enclosed a copy of the billing records you have requested. However, please be aware that our centralized billingteam does not have access to the medical records.

To obtain treatment/medical records, please submit a request directly to the treating facility and/or facilities. If you have already submitted a request for treatment/medical records directly to the treating facility, those records will arrive under separate cover. :
